channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: American woman missing in Bahamas as husband injured during police custody transfer
item.description: Brian Hooker, a 59-year-old man from Michigan, was injured after falling overboard while being transported by police in the Bahamas, days following the disappearance of his wife, Lynette Hooker. Accor...

item.title: Florida AG Launches Investigation into OpenAI Over Safety and Security Concerns
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier announced an investigation into OpenAI, citing concerns about potential harm to minors, national security risks, and a possible connection to a fatal shooting a...

item.title: Pakistan’s Strategic Role as a Mediator Between Iran and the United States
item.description: The article by Ashok Swain explores how Pakistan has emerged as the key mediator between Iran and the United States amid the ongoing West Asian crisis. It argues that this development is not an unexpe...

item.title: Psychological Impact on Mother and Baby After Infant Abduction in South Africa
item.description: On 11 April 2025, a newborn in Hillbrow was abducted from a mother who had been assisted by two women during her clinic visits. The women gained her trust, offered money for food, and convinced her to...

item.title: Guidance and fairness key in 2026 university and college placements
item.description: As the process of applying for university and college courses gets underway, the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has officially opened the application portal for the...

item.title: Democrats Consider 25th Amendment to Remove Trump Amid Renewed Concerns Over Fitness for Office
item.description: The article examines renewed calls from Democratic lawmakers in the United States to invoke the 25th Amendment of the Constitution to remove President Donald Trump from office, citing growing concerns...

item.title: Revisiting Malcolm X: Lessons for South Africa’s Post-Colonial Democracy
item.description: Professor Itumeleng Daniel Mothoagae reflects on Malcolm X’s 1964 speech, 'The Ballot or the Bullet,' and explores how its message still resonates within the post-colonial landscape of South Africa an...

item.title: Key Events in Latin America and the Caribbean: Protests, Campaigns and Holy Week Observances
item.description: During the week of 3–9 April 2026, Latin America and the Caribbean witnessed a mixture of cultural, political, and social events. In Atlixco, Mexico, penitents marked Good Friday by wearing chains in ...

item.title: Karen Webb Cleared of Misconduct in Gin Gifts Scandal
item.description: Karen Webb, the former NSW Police commissioner, has been cleared of serious misconduct after a lengthy investigation by the Law Enforcement Conduct Commission (LECC) regarding her involvement in a tax...

item.title: Review of 'Everybody Loves Sohrab Handa': An Engaging Ensemble Murder Mystery
item.description: Rajat Kapoor’s latest Hindi film, 'Everybody Loves Sohrab Handa', streaming on ZEE5, offers a cosy murder mystery set in a charming cottage in Khandala. The story unfolds during a wedding anniversary ...

item.title: How Australian Theme Parks Ensure Roller Coaster Safety
item.description: During the school holidays, a major roller coaster at Warner Bros Movie World on the Gold Coast halted mid-ride, though all passengers disembarked safely. This incident highlights ongoing concerns abo...

item.title: Professor explains how Filipinos fall victim to widespread disinformation despite being critical thinkers
item.description: In a recent discussion reported by ABS-CBN News, a communication professor highlighted that Filipinos should not be branded as gullible, but instead be seen as victims of an organized ecosystem of dis...

item.title: Measles Outbreak in South Carolina Puts Infants at Risk Amid Falling Vaccination Rates
item.description: A recent measles outbreak in South Carolina has highlighted the risks faced by infants too young to receive the MMR vaccine. The outbreak, now the largest in the U.S. in over 35 years, prompted some d...

item.title: Seven Northern States to Experience Scheduled Power Outages for Jos-Gombe Transmission Line Upgrade
item.description: My people, make una hear this one o. From April 9 to May 22, 2026, electricity consumers for seven Northern states go dey face serious power cuts as dem dey upgrade the national grid. The affected sta...

item.title: Toronto Police Charge Excavating Company Owner in GFL Executive Shootings
item.description: Toronto police have laid charges against Ilan Philosophe, the 42-year-old owner of a local excavating and shoring company, in connection with a shooting incident targeting executives of GFL Environmen...

item.title: Challenges in Negotiating a Long-Term Peace Deal with Iran After Ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, was a dramatic shift, coming after days of escalating threats. However, its sustainability is uncertain, as regional rivalries and s...

item.title: PIA Plans Gradual Fleet Expansion Amid Economic Stability
item.description: KARACHI: Arif Habib, chairman of the Arif Habib Consortium which owns Pakistan International Airlines (PIA), has stated that the national carrier currently has 30 aircraft, with only 18 operational an...

item.title: B.C. Man Faces Challenges With ICBC Over Steering Wheel Replacement
item.description: A British Columbia man, Darrin Penner, is fighting against ICBC after his 2005 Ford F-350 Harley Davidson Edition was damaged during a theft attempt. Thieves tried to steal the truck and cut through a...

item.title: Markets remain steady despite geopolitical tensions
item.description: Australian and global financial markets have shown remarkable resilience despite a week of geopolitical tension, including threats from US President Donald Trump. Australian shares have returned to ne...

item.title: Albert Mazibuko bids farewell after 55 years with Ladysmith Black Mambazo
item.description: Albert Mazibuko, the veteran South African singer and long-standing member of Ladysmith Black Mambazo, passed away aged 77. He is celebrated for his remarkable contribution to the internationally reno...
